notes: Examine.com evidence-based summary aggregating peer-reviewed research on coffee; covers cognitive, metabolic, and disease-risk outcomes with citations

Poole et al. 2017 BMJ umbrella review of 201 meta-analyses: coffee associated with reduced risk of T2DM, CVD, several cancers, and neurodegenerative disease; most evidence favors benefit at 3-4 cups/day
Yan et al. 2025 Public Health Nutrition — NHANES cohort 43k participants; moderate coffee (1-2 cups/day) associated with ~2 year gain in life expectancy at age 50 vs non-drinkers
Xiao et al. 2025 European Heart Journal — 40k NHANES adults; morning coffee pattern associated with 16% lower all-cause mortality and 31% lower CVD mortality vs non-drinkers; timing matters
